_comment: The casa-start stage interview, in FOUR passes: Define (what/who), Locate (stage tier), Core (north star, do-or-die constraint, horizon, anti-priorities, funding), and Backfill (skipped foundations). A greenfield run covers Define+Locate+Core+Backfill in about 6 to 9 interactions: the skill infers the low-yield Define answers from the one-liner and confirms them in one batch, skipping any question whose ask_when is unmet. An EXISTING-project run lets scan.mjs + the project-scanner pre-fill Define/Locate, so those passes are confirm-only and the budget is spent on the Core pass + a gap confirmation (about 4 to 6 interactions). The skill assembles the answers object { type, secondary_type, company_name, one_liner, icp, monetization, traits, tier, gaps, north_star_archetype, constraint_archetype, win_definition, horizon, anti_priorities } and feeds it to scripts/stage.mjs, which deterministically derives the profile, start level, and seed, AND seeds the initial business-aware pulse (pulse.json) from the Core answers. Each option's `sets` records the engine signal it produces; traits must stay within the catalog vocabulary (stage.mjs validates this). Keep prompts plain: no em-dashes, no emojis.
_scan_keys: Per-question scan-awareness, read only on an existing-project run. satisfied_by_signal: a deterministic scan.mjs signal that proves this answer; with mode_when_scanned 'skip' the skill takes the scanned value and does not ask. prefilled_by: a project-scanner inferred field (for example inferred.type) that proposes an answer for the founder to confirm. mode_when_scanned: 'confirm' (include in the single batched confirmation the founder eyeballs) or 'skip' (accept silently, the code proved it). When neither a signal nor a prefill is present, the question is asked normally; a greenfield run always asks.

passes.intent: What the business is and who it serves. Sets the static traits that decide which playbooks are members. On an existing-project run the scan + project-scanner pre-fill this whole pass; the skill confirms in one batch and never asks it cold.

questions.prompt: In one line, what is the business and who is it for?
Which best describes it?
Who pays you?
How do deals close?

options.label: High value, sales-led (roughly 10k+ per year per account)

questions.prompt: How does it make money?
Are you building software?
Is your audience technical (developers or engineers)?
Do you store customer or personal data (accounts, emails, payment info)?
Do you send email to your users or customers (welcome, transactional, or marketing)?
Is this a local, in-person service (served in one area, not sold online nationally)?
Are you actively running paid acquisition right now (ads on Google, Meta, TikTok, and similar)?
Will AI agents run parts of this company autonomously, beyond you using AI tools yourself?

passes.intent: Where the business is today. Sets the stage tier, which fixes the start level and the milestone state-flags, and decides how much work is pre-seeded as done. The scan can prove building/launched, but code can never prove paying customers, so a revenue tier always needs founder confirmation (revenue-truth).

questions.prompt: Where are you today?

options.label: Landing page or waitlist up, no product yet
Building the product, not launched (have a repo or a deploy)
Launched, have users, no revenue yet
Product-market fit, scaling on paid channels

questions.revenue_truth: Code cannot prove paying customers. If the proposed tier is launched, revenue, or scaling, the founder must confirm it; never set a revenue tier from the scan alone.

passes.intent: What matters to THIS founder right now. This pass is never scannable: only the founder can name their north star, their do-or-die constraint, their horizon, and what they are deliberately not doing. stage.mjs apply reads these answers to seed the initial business-aware pulse (pulse.json), so the very first build map already leans toward the founder's goal before any manual pulse cascade. Always run this pass, greenfield or existing.

questions.prompt: What is the one number that, if it goes up, means this is working? In your words.
questions.guidance: Offer the chips suggested for this business type as a prompt, then map the founder's free-text answer to exactly one canonical archetype id. The chips mirror the north-star families in scripts/northstar.mjs so they match the business type; the founder may also describe something off-menu, which you still map to the closest single id.

archetypes.metrics: activation rate, time to first value, percent of signups that reach the aha moment
retention, net revenue retention, DAU/MAU stickiness, repeat use, rebooking
MRR, ARR, net revenue, booked revenue, protocol revenue
signups, new users, MAU, DAU, active wallets, top of funnel
visitor to signup, trial to paid, checkout conversion
GMV, match rate, transaction volume, TVL, take volume
LTV to CAC, gross margin, ARPU, payback period

chip_overrides.note: A single-location in-person service steers by bookings, rebooking, and reviews.
Subscription businesses steer by MRR or ARR; lead with revenue_mrr.
Transaction and take-rate businesses steer by volume; lead with gmv_liquidity.

questions.prompt: What is the single thing most likely to kill this, the do-or-die constraint right now?
questions.guidance: Map the founder's free-text answer to exactly one canonical constraint id. This becomes a department weight delta in the initial pulse (for example regulatory_legal lifts Legal), so pick the closest single id.

archetypes.meaning: not enough people are using it
people use it but are not paying, or revenue is too small
running low on cash or spending too fast
compliance, licensing, or legal exposure blocks progress
the product breaks or cannot handle the load
not enough hands to do the work

questions.prompt: What would make the next stretch a clear win? If you can, give the metric, where it is now, and the target (for example: weekly active users, 50 now, 1000 target).
questions.guidance: When the founder names a metric WITH a current and target number, set win_definition to a STRUCTURED object { metric_id, current_value (number), target_value (number), deadline (optional, from the horizon answer) }. The engine turns this into a win_gap that sharpens how hard the binding constraint's plays are pushed (a company far from target gets its constraint work pushed harder than one nearly there). If they only give a qualitative answer, set win_definition to that string. Do not invent numbers the founder did not give.

questions.prompt: Over what horizon?
What are you deliberately NOT working on yet? Name anything you want Casa to hold back.
questions.guidance: Map the founder's free text to the playbook ids they want demoted, where you can name them confidently (for example a founder saying 'no fundraising yet' maps to pre-seed-fundraise). These become demote weights in the initial pulse. If nothing maps cleanly, leave anti_priorities as an empty array; do not invent ids.

questions.prompt: How are you funding this?

options.label: Raising or plan to raise venture capital

questions.note: Ask only if funding is not already known. Code cannot prove funding, so the project-scanner puts it in ask_founder; if the founder already stated it, skip this question.

passes.intent: For an existing business, find the foundational work that was skipped so it is not wrongly marked done. Anything selected here is left out of the seed and surfaces as a ready catch-up item at the current level. The level floor keeps the company from regressing. On an existing-project run the project-scanner reconciles only the four high-value unblockers it can read (analytics, payments, legal/ToS, deploy) plus any gap it flagged; do not ask the whole battery cold.

questions.prompt: Which of these have you NOT done yet? Select all that apply.

options.label: Published terms of service and a privacy policy
Raised a pre-seed or seed round

questions.scanned_reconcile_note: On an existing-project run, confirm done-vs-gap with the founder for only the four high-value unblockers the scanner can read: analytics (analytics-stack-setup), payments (the takes_payments truth and monetization), legal/ToS (tos-and-privacy-policy), and deploy (hosting-deployment-setup). A present dependency proves the wiring exists, not that it is done correctly, so reconcile rather than assume. Do not ask the entity / domain / security / fundraise items cold unless the scanner flagged one.
